data.title: Vistance Networks Shares Gain on $1.85B Sale of Ruckus Networks to Belden
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Shares of Vistance Networks climbed after the company agreed to sell its Ruckus Networks business to Belden for $1.85 billion.

Shares were up 24% to $12.98 as of midday Thursday. The stock has fallen 28% this year.

Belden said Ruckus would add a leading network equipment and solutions provider to its portfolio for enterprise and industrial customers. It said it has fully committed debt financing from JPMorgan.

The deal is expected to close in the second half of this year.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Axalta Coating Systems Ltd. 1Q 2026: Revenue $1.25B, EPS $0.42— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Axalta Coating Systems Ltd. reported first-quarter 2026 results with net sales of $1.25B, net income of $90M and diluted EPS of $0.42, compared with $1.26B, $99M and $0.45 in the year‑ago quarter.

Financial Highlights

MetricCurrent quarterPrior year quarterYoY change
Revenue¹$1.25B$1.26B(0.6%)
Net income²$90M$99M(9.1%)
Diluted EPS³$0.42$0.45(6.7%)

¹ Reported as “Net sales”. ² Reported as “Net income attributable to common shareholders”. ³ Reported as “Diluted net income per share”.

Business Highlights

  • Net sales slipped 0.6% year over year to $1,254M, driven by a 6.2% volume decline partially offset by foreign exchange and recent acquisitions.
  • By segment, Performance Coatings sales fell 2.4% amid volume and price/mix headwinds; Mobility Coatings rose 2.8%, aided by FX and price/mix improvements.
  • Lower variable input costs and ongoing savings initiatives helped margins in Mobility, while freight and mix pressures weighed on Performance.
  • Axalta operates 42 manufacturing plants and four technology centers, with about 12,300 employees and distribution in more than 140 countries through 5,000+ distributors.
  • The company has entered an all‑stock merger agreement with AkzoNobel and is monitoring commodity and supply risks related to Middle East conflict developments.

Original SEC Filing: Axalta Coating Systems Ltd. [ AXTA ] - 10-Q - Apr. 30, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: U.S. Economy Grew at 2% Rate in First Quarter — WSJ
data.description:
By Harriet Torry

U.S. economic growth picked up in the first quarter as businesses invested heavily in artificial intelligence, rebounding from a fourth quarter dented by a government shutdown.

At the same time, the economy didn't expand as fast as economists expected, weighed down by softer consumer spending growth.

The numbers

The Commerce Department said U.S. gross domestic product — the value of all goods and services produced across the economy — rose at a seasonally and inflation adjusted 2% annual rate in the first quarter.

Economists surveyed by The Wall Street Journal had expected GDP growth of 2.2% for the January to March period.

Consumer and business spending

Consumer spending, the economy's main engine, rose at a 1.6% pace in the first quarter, slower than the 1.9% pace in the fourth quarter of last year.

Businesses picked up their spending, with nonresidential fixed investment rising at a 10.4% rate. Spending on intellectual property and equipment was particularly strong, a sign companies continued to invest heavily in artificial intelligence.

"What you have here is an AI-inspired GDP increase, that in coming quarters will see a drag," due to the Iran war," said Joseph Brusuelas, chief economist at RSM.

Under the hood

A measure of underlying demand in the economy strengthened thanks to the strong business investment, and despite uncertainties around the war in Iran and the Trump administration's tariff policies.

Final sales to private domestic purchasers rose at a 2.5% rate in the first quarter, picking up from 1.8% in the prior quarter. The measure carves out the more volatile government, inventory and international trade data.

Shutdown payback

Spending by the federal government rose at a 9.3% rate, picking up strongly from a 16.6% contraction in the prior quarter. The fourth-quarter drag was due to the record-long government shutdown, which ended in November.

Trade

Net exports, a measure of what the country exports minus what it imports, were a drag on growth in the first quarter, subtracting 1.3 percentage point as imports outpaced export. Trade caused wild swings in GDP readings over the course of 2025. The Trump administration enacted sweeping tariffs on imports, causing companies to stockpile — and then rein in — shipments of imported goods. The Supreme Court struck down those tariffs in February.

Where things stand

The GDP report is the first major economic scorecard for the January-to-March quarter.

The U.S. economy entered the year on a steady, albeit weaker, footing. Growth had slowed sharply in the fourth quarter of 2025, dented by the government shutdown. The economy shed jobs over the final three months of the year, although payroll growth has picked up since then, averaging 68,000 jobs a month in the first quarter.

High gas prices, continuing conflict

Thursday's data also offers a partial look at how the conflict in Iran has affected spending. The U.S. and Israel launched attacks on Iran on the last day of February, and the average price of regular gasoline has jumped about 44% since then.

The quarterly GDP report doesn't capture any fallout from continued fuel-price hikes in April, when regular gasoline averaged about $4.10 a gallon nationwide. In April, the University of Michigan's consumer-sentiment index dropped to its lowest reading on record due to angst about the war and prices.

No rate cuts in sight

Measures of inflation and inflation expectations have also picked up in recent months, complicating the pathway for rate cuts this year by the Federal Reserve. Investors started the year expecting two rate cuts in 2026; they now see a more than 80% probability the Fed will keep interest rates on hold through December, according to CME Group data.

The GDP report came a day after Fed officials wrapped up their latest policy meeting. They voted Wednesday to hold the short-term interest-rate target steady, citing elevated inflation and low job gains.

"Developments in the Middle East are contributing to a high level of uncertainty about the economic outlook," the Fed's rate-setting Federal Open Market Committee said.

Companies are reporting solid earnings...

Major companies have largely reported strong sales and earnings in recent weeks, a sign that demand remains steady. Consumers are benefiting from higher tax refunds this filing season and are supported by a still-low unemployment rate.

Even so, some companies caution that shoppers are feeling skittish about the Iran war and high gasoline prices.

...But their customers are downbeat

Domino's Pizza on Monday revised down its U.S. same-store sales growth estimates. The chain said sales softened, with lower-income shoppers particularly pulling back.

Procter & Gamble, long a bellwether for the health of the U.S. consumer economy, last week reported higher quarterly sales but warned geopolitical uncertainty and political tensions are weighing on shoppers.

"The consumer is still a little bit muted," said Andre Schulten, P&G's chief financial officer, although larger tax refunds were a "bright spot" for Americans that boosted disposable income.

Homepage photo credit: Michael Nagle/Bloomberg News

Write to Harriet Torry at harriet.torry@wsj.com

data.title: Quanta Services Shares Hit 52-Week High With 1Q Earnings Beat, Lifted Outlook
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Shares of Quanta Services rose after the company reported first-quarter earnings ahead of analyst expectations and raised its outlook for the full year.

Shares were up 12% to $707.05 in midday Thursday trading after hitting a 52-week high of $727.25 earlier in the session. The stock is up 60% year to date.

The Houston-based infrastructure solutions company on Thursday reported a profit of $220.6 million, or $1.45 a share, compared with a profit of $144.3 million, or 96 cents a share, a year earlier.

Stripping out certain one-time items, adjusted earnings were $2.68 a share. Analysts polled by FactSet were expecting $2.04 a share.

Revenue rose to $7.87 billion, up from $6.23 billion. Analysts were expecting $7 billion in revenue.

The company said it now expects full-year adjusted earnings between $13.55 and $14.25 a share on revenue between $34.7 billion and $35.2 billion. It had previously forecast adjusted earnings of $12.65 to $13.35 a share on revenue between $33.25 billion and $33.75 billion.

Analysts are expecting adjusted earnings of $13.08 a share on revenue of $33.42 billion.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: L3Harris Raises Outlook on Strong Defense Demand, Advances Missile Spinoff — Update
data.description:
By Connor Hart

L3Harris Technologies raised its full-year outlook after stronger demand for weapons and defense systems boosted earnings and revenue in the latest quarter.

The defense contractor also on Thursday took steps toward spinning off its missile-solutions business, naming the new company and confidentially filing a draft registration statement with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

"The global security environment is evolving rapidly, and the implications for our customers are increasingly clear," Chief Executive Chris Kubasik said. "Across the Middle East, Europe and the Indo-Pacific, the threat environment is driving greater urgency around readiness, resilience and modernization."

The heightened demand is reflected in U.S. efforts to ramp up missile production and increase defense spending. The Pentagon has struck deals with defense companies, including a $1 billion investment in L3Harris' missile business. It has separately partnered with Lockheed Martin to more than triple production of Patriot interceptors to roughly 2,000 missiles annually.

The new missile company will be named AXYV, inspired by missile guidance engineering, Kubasik said on a call with analysts. "The A refers to the axes of X and Y, and the V to the velocity of the missile trajectory."

L3Harris in January unveiled plans to spin off the missile segment into a separate publicly traded company. It will retain a controlling interest in the business, which makes rocket components for U.S. and allied systems, including Patriot, Thaad and Tomahawk missiles. The spinoff is expected in the second half of 2026.

L3Harris said it now expects full-year adjusted earnings of $11.40 to $11.60 a share, up from a prior outlook of $11.30 to $11.50. It reaffirmed its revenue forecast of $23 billion to $23.5 billion. Analysts polled by FactSet expect adjusted earnings of $11.59 a share on revenue of $23.44 billion.

For the first quarter, L3Harris reported profit of $512 million, compared with $386 million a year earlier. Earnings of $2.72 a share topped analysts' expectations of $2.53 a share.

Revenue rose 12% to $5.74 billion, exceeding Wall Street estimates of $5.42 billion.

The company's space and mission systems segment generated $2.99 billion in revenue, up 24% from a year earlier. Its communication systems unit posted revenue of $1.86 billion, up 2.5%.

Revenue in the missile solutions segment increased 18% to $990 million.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: Buda Juice Announces First Quarter Fiscal 2026 Financial Results Conference Call and Webcast
data.description:
DALLAS--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 30, 2026--

Buda Juice, Inc. (NYSE American: BUDA), $BUDA, pioneer of the new UltraFresh(TM) juice category for the supermarket fresh produce department with its cold-crafted, UltraFresh(TM) lemonades and citrus juices, today announced it intends host a conference call on Wednesday, May 13, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. Eastern Time to discuss its financial and operational results for its first quarter ended March 31, 2026.

The Company expects to issue its first quarter fiscal 2026 earnings press release prior to the market open on May 13, 2026. A live webcast of the conference call will also be available on the Investor Relations section of the Company's website at https://budajuice.com/investors.

 
Dial-in and Webcast Information 
-------------------------------- 
                                     Wednesday, May 13, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. 
Date/Time:                           Eastern Time 
Toll-Free (North America):           (800) 715-9871 
Toll/International:                  (646) 307-1963 
Conference Call ID:                  7817249 
 
                                     https://events.q4inc.com/attendee/60530 
Webcast Link:                        2226 
 
Replay Information 
-------------------------------- 
Toll-Free (North America)            (800) 770-2030 
Toll/International:                  (609) 800-9909 
Conference Call ID:                  7817249 
Expiration:                          Wednesday, May 20, 2026, 11:59 p.m. 
                                     Eastern Time

About Buda Juice

Buda Juice (NYSE American: BUDA) is the creator and pioneer of the Ultra Fresh(TM) category through an end-to-end cold chain platform that delivers always-cold, freshly crafted juices, lemonades and wellness shots to grocery retailers. The Company provides a turnkey alternative to shelf-stable beverages and in-store juicing, enabling retailers to offer truly fresh, clean-label products without added infrastructure or operational complexity. Its continuous 35degF cold chain from fruit to shelf delivers products with an 8 to 12-day shelf life that preserves authentic taste and nutrient quality while enabling efficient retail distribution.

Buda Juice's proprietary cold-chain infrastructure enables the Company to scale the Ultra Fresh category nationally while maintaining the quality, safety and consistency required by leading grocery retailers, with a disciplined focus on profitability. Additional information about the Company can be found at https://budajuice.com.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260430855803/en/

CONTACT: Buda Juice Investor/Press Contact:

Brian S. Siegel, IRC(R), M.B.A.

Senior Managing Director

Hayden IR - Chicago

(346) 396-8696 (o)

brian@haydenir.com

data.title: Thomson Reuters Cleared to Proceed With $605M Special Distribution, Reverse Split
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

Thomson Reuters received court approval for a special return of capital of $605 million and a go-ahead for a share consolidation.

The Canadian information and technology company said Thursday that the Ontario Superior Court of Justice issued its final order approving the plan of arrangement covering both the cash distribution and the reverse stock split.

The special payout amounts to roughly $1.36 a share, with the final figure to be set once the company determines how many shareholders are participating.

Thomson Reuters also will proceed with a consolidation of its outstanding shares on a proportional basis. The company expects to finalize the distribution amount and the consolidation ratio before the plan of arrangement takes effect on May 4.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com
